heroHeadline: IDF Probe: Kibbutz Holit Left Undefended for 7 Hours on Oct 7; High Court Extends Government's October 7 Inquiry Deadline — Day 200 Ceasefire
heroSubtitle: The deadliest attack on Jewish people since the Holocaust — assault timeline, hostage crisis resolved Jan 2026, intelligence failures, and IDF response. On April 27, 2026 (Day 200 of the ceasefire), the IDF publicly released its final probe into the October 7 attack on Kibbutz Holit, concluding the community was left undefended for 6 hours and 53 minutes against ~60 Hamas Nukhba terrorists — 13 civilians and 3 IDF soldiers were killed, and 6 residents abducted to Gaza (4 returned; 2 murdered in captivity). Kibbutz residents rejected the findings and demanded a state commission. Separately, Israel's High Court of Justice granted the government an additional two months — until late June 2026 — to decide the form of an October 7 investigation, declining to immediately mandate a state commission after a chaotic April 23 hearing. The cumulative death toll since October 7, 2023 now exceeds 72,589 killed and 172,375 injured per WAFA and Gaza Health Ministry. Phase Two disarmament negotiations remain in deep deadlock; all three Board of Peace deadlines have passed without Hamas acceptance.
footerNote: Data sourced from IDF, Israel Police, UN OCHA, Israeli government, Reuters, AP, Haaretz, WAFA, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are official Israeli counts; Hamas-theater figures are separately noted and contested. All 251 hostages accounted for as of January 26, 2026. Cumulative conflict dead (Oct 7, 2023 – Apr 2026): 72,589+ Palestinians per OCHA/Gaza MoH/WAFA. Ceasefire in effect since Oct 2025; 2,400+ violations documented by Gaza GMO — Palestinians killed since ceasefire: 814+ as of Apr 26, 2026. Gaza reconstruction cost: $71.4B per UN-EU-World Bank joint assessment (Apr 21, 2026). Apr 27: IDF releases final Holit probe — kibbutz left without IDF reinforcement for 6h53m on Oct 7.
